nodes.name: LC
nodes.summary: LC表示 IUCN 无危等级，当前关联8208种鸟类，如鸵鸟、鸸鹋、南方鶴鴕等8208种鸟类。

nodes.name: Samuel Washington Woodhouse
nodes.summary: Samuel Washington Woodhouse是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联2种鸟类，包括白喉雨燕、Black-capped vireo，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 雨燕科
nodes.summary: 雨燕科（Apodidae）是图谱中的科级分类节点，当前关联78种鸟类，如Chestnut-collared swift、Tepui swift、White-collared swift等78种鸟类。

nodes.name: 北美洲
nodes.summary: 北美洲是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联97种鸟类，包括紅嘴樹鴨、黑嘴天鹅、加拿大雁等97种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 斯波坎縣
nodes.summary: 斯波坎縣是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联1种鸟类，包括白喉雨燕，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 农药污染
nodes.summary: 农业化学品通过水体和食物链影响鸟类生存。

nodes.name: 白喉雨燕
nodes.summary: 白喉雨燕（White-throated swift，学名 Aeronautes saxatalis），分类上属于雨燕目，并归入雨燕科，在图谱中标记为无危（LC）物种，已关联5个分布地点节点，常见栖息地包括峡谷峭壁，主要威胁包括农药污染。

nodes.name: 大盆地
nodes.summary: 大盆地是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联4种鸟类，包括石鸡、白喉雨燕、American bushtit等4种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 峡谷峭壁
nodes.summary: 大型猛禽和食腐鸟偏好的高崖栖息环境。

nodes.name: 洪都拉斯
nodes.summary: 洪都拉斯是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联132种鸟类，包括山冠雉、White-bellied chachalaca、茶臉鶉等132种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 雨燕目
nodes.summary: 雨燕目（Apodiformes）是图谱中的目级分类节点，当前关联177种鸟类，如Starry owlet-nightjar、Wallace's owlet-nightjar、Mountain owlet-nightjar等177种鸟类。

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: The white-throated swift (Aeronautes saxatalis) is a swift of the family Apodidae native to western North America, south to cordilleran western Honduras.Ryan TP, Collins CT

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: Its coastal range extends as far north as Northern California, while inland it has migratory populations found throughout the Great Basin and Rocky Mountain regions, ranging as far north as southern British Columbia. White-throated swifts are found in open areas near cliffs, rock faces, or man-made structures, where they roost

links.label: 栖息于
links.evidence: Its coastal range extends as far north as Northern California, while inland it has migratory populations found throughout the Great Basin and Rocky Mountain regions, ranging as far north as southern British Columbia. White-throated swifts are found in open areas near cliffs, rock faces, or man-made structures, where they roost

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: Across North America, white-throated swift populations have declined by 2.8% from 1966–1998, which is considered to be a significant decline. Such declines could be related to loss of roosting and nesting sites from quarrying, mining, and the demolition of older buildings, as well as reduction in food supply due to pesticide use. However, in certain areas swift populations appear to have increasing trends, and most populations appear stable. The patchy distribution of this species makes interpreting these data difficult, but the apparent overall decline is of concern given the concurrent declines of other aerial insectivores
